changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforcement to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_required for clarity
Changed k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled to k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enforced for consistency
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they map to security controls related to secure information flow. However, minor naming adjustments were made for clarity and consistency. The original names were mostly accurate but could benefit from slight refinements to better align with Kubernetes terminology and ensure clarity in what the checks enforce.
naming_improvements: Use 'tls_required' instead of 'tls_enforcement' for network policies to specify the requirement clearly.
Use 'mtls_enforced' instead of 'mtls_enabled' to indicate the enforcement nature of the control.
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | filter mechanisms using multiple processes when t
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(30) focuses on enforcing secure information flow through mechanisms like client certificates and secure transport policies. In Kubernetes, similar security controls can be enforced through NetworkPolicies to ensure secure communication, enabling TLS for Ingress resources, enforcing mTLS in service meshes, and validating TLS certificates via admission controllers.
